Certain Conditions Originating in the Pe.Definition: Perinatal period = before birth through the 28th day following birth.

Use of Chapter 16 Codes.rule: Never used on maternal record; Chapter 15 codes never used on newborn record.
Use of Chapter 16 Codes.lifelong_use: May be used throughout life if condition persists.

Principal Diagnosis for Birth Record.rule: Assign Z38 code as principal for birth episode; used only once at birth and only on newborn record.
Principal Diagnosis for Birth Record.transfer_rule: Do not use Z38 at receiving hospital for transferred newborn.

Use of Codes from other Chapters with Ch.rule: Allowed if provides more specific detail.
Use of Codes from other Chapters with Ch.sequencing: If reason for encounter is perinatal condition, Chapter 16 code is sequenced first.

General Perinatal Rules.Use of Chapter 16 Codes after the Perina: Continue using perinatal code regardless of age if condition originated in perinatal period.

Birth process or community acquired cond.rule: If unclear, default to birth process and assign Chapter 16 code.
Birth process or community acquired cond.exception: If clearly community-acquired, do not assign Chapter 16 code.

criteria: Has implications for future health care needs

Use of Z05 codes.rule: Assign Z05 if healthy newborn evaluated for suspected condition ruled out.
Use of Z05 codes.exclusion: Do not use if signs/symptoms present — code the sign/symptom instead.

Observation and Evaluation of Newborns f.Z05 on other than the birth record: May be principal/first-listed for readmissions when Z38 no longer applies; only for healthy newborns with no confirmed condition.

Coding Additional Perinatal Diagnoses.Conditions requiring treatment: Assign codes for conditions requiring treatment, investigation, longer stay, or resource use.
Coding Additional Perinatal Diagnoses.Implications for future health care need: Assign if provider documents such implications (not applicable for adults).

Prematurity and Fetal Growth Retardation.based_on: Recorded birth weight and estimated gestational age.
Prematurity and Fetal Growth Retardation.dual_coding: If both available, assign two P07 codes (birth weight first, then gestational age).

Low birth weight and immaturity status.rule: P07 codes may be used for any age if prematurity/low birth weight affects current health.

Bacterial Sepsis of Newborn.rule: Category P36 includes congenital sepsis; default to congenital if not specified.

causal_organism.included_in_code: Do not assign B95-B96 if organism in P36 code.

Bacterial Sepsis of Newborn.additional_codes: Add R65.2- and organ dysfunction codes if severe.

Stillbirth.code: P95 only in institutions with separate stillbirth records.
Stillbirth.restrictions: No other code with P95; do not use on mother's record.

COVID-19 Infection in Newborn.default: If no transmission type documented, assign U07.1 and manifestation codes.
COVID-19 Infection in Newborn.in_utero_or_birth: If documented as contracted in utero/birth, assign P35.8 + U07.1.
COVID-19 Infection in Newborn.birth_episode: Always assign appropriate Z38 as principal for birth episode.
